special_mechanics.name: Vehicle Transformation
special_mechanics.description: Player can convert car into speedboat for brief periods by driving through a boathouse. Weapons change in water mode: oil slick becomes flamethrower, smoke screen and missiles remain unchanged.

special_mechanics.name: Gear Shifting
special_mechanics.description: Two-position stick shift offering 'low' and 'high' gears for speed control

special_mechanics.name: Special Weapons System
special_mechanics.description: Limited ammunition weapons accessed via weapons van: oil slicks, smoke screens, surface-to-air missiles. Each weapon controlled by different button with dashboard light indicators.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Empirical/qualitative data only. Source documents (TCRF, Wikipedia) contain development history, gameplay mechanics, and feature descriptions but no precise physics values, movement speeds, gravity constants, or sub-pixel system information. Game is vehicle combat simulator, not character platformer, so traditional platforming physics (jump, gravity, etc.) do not apply. Movement controlled via steering wheel, acceleration pedal, and gear shift.
sources_used: wikipedia - Spy Hunter article (covers series including NES Super Spy Hunter)
